text.title: Rate-Raise Conversation Script Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Rate-Raise Conversation Script application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Raise rates annually OR on scope expansion. Never twice a year; never on a whim.
rule.rationale: Annual cadence sets expectation; ad-hoc raises feel arbitrary + erode trust.

rule.statement: Standard raise: 10-25% per year (depending on inflation + market). <10% = ineffective; >25% triggers churn.
rule.rationale: Industry data: 10-25% is the durable band; outside provokes immediate friction.

rule.statement: Script anchors on value delivered (specific outcomes + numbers), then states new rate, then offers 30-60 day transition.
rule.rationale: Value-anchored + transition window converts most pushback; price-only conversation prompts negotiation.

rule.statement: Pre-script 3 responses: (a) 'we can't pay more' → propose scope reduction at same rate; (b) 'half raise' → counter at full; (c) 'we'll find someone else' → walk politely.
rule.rationale: Negotiation success rate doubles when responses are pre-scripted; ad-hoc responses cave under pressure.

rule.statement: Pre-decide the walk-away threshold (e.g., '<15% raise = exit'). Sticking to threshold preserves rate floor.
rule.rationale: Without pre-commitment, founders accept partial raises that lock in below-market rates.

rule.statement: If all Applies-If preconditions hold and no Skip-If disqualifier is present, execute the full procedure from `04-procedure.xml` and commit the artefact described in `02-output-contract.xml`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's affirmative leaf; pairs the preconditions with the methodology's commit path.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If condition is present (one-shot, <3 instances/year, regulator override, missing owner), do NOT apply this methodology. Route to the sibling/upstream methodology named in `AGENTS.md` Related section.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's skip leaf; prevents over-application of a heavyweight artefact when the loop conditions do not hold.
